text: Meeting deadlines is an essential skill in both study and work. Good time management helps you break large tasks into smaller, manageable steps and schedule them over days or weeks. Prioritization is equally important: identify which tasks are urgent or carry the most weight, and do those first. Scope management means avoiding unnecessary additions to your work—if you keep expanding the task, you may miss the deadline. Finally, open communication with your manager or teammates allows you to ask for help or negotiate extra time if needed. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and consistently deliver quality work on time.
